access: the right or way to get in
across: from one side to the other
act: to do, or to play a part
address: where a house is, in words
adjective: a word that describes a thing
adverb: a word that describes a verb
afford: to have enough money for it
along: from one end towards the other
an: "a" before a, e, i, o, u
animal: a living thing, not a plant
answer: what you say to a question
any: one, it does not matter which
application: a written request for a job
apply: to ask for a job, or to use
assistant: a person who helps at work
at: in one exact place or time
atmosphere: the air, or the feeling of a place
basketball: a game with a ball and net
beat: to win against, or to hit
bill: the paper saying what you must pay
blog: a personal page on the internet
blood: the red liquid in your body
board: a flat surface, or a group of leaders
body: all of a person, head to foot
borrow: to take and give back later
brazil: the biggest country in South America
break: to make into pieces, or a rest
breakfast: the first meal of the day
bus: a big vehicle for many people
button: you press it or fasten it
by: who did it, or next to
canada: a country north of the USA
capital: the main city, or A B C
careful: giving attention so nothing goes wrong
cat: a small pet that says meow
catch: to take hold of something moving
charge: the price, or to fill with power
check: to look if it is right
clause: part of a sentence with a verb
clearly: in a way easy to see
climate: the usual weather of a place
clock: it shows the time on a wall
club: a group meeting for one thing
column: a tall narrow part of a page
comfortable: nice to sit or be in
compare: to see how two things differ
complain: to say you are not happy
computer: a machine for work and internet
control: to make it do what you want
copy: one made the same as another
council: a group that runs a town
country: a land with its own laws
credit: money you can use and pay later
degree: a level, or a university title
describe: to say what it is like
difference: how two things are not the same
disappointed: sad it was not as hoped
door: you open it to go in
drink: to take liquid in your mouth
eat: to put food in your mouth
every: all of them, one by one
evidence: facts that show it is true
example: one thing that shows the rest
excuse: a word to get attention, or a reason
expression: a set way of saying something
factory: a building where things are made
fashion: the clothes that are popular now
field: open land, or an area of study
find: to see what you looked for
fine: all right, or money you must pay
fit: to be the right size, or healthy
flat: rooms you live in, in a block
floor: you walk on it, or a storey
flower: the pretty part of a plant
football: a game with a ball and feet
for: shows who or what gets it
forget: to lose it from your mind
free: no money needed, or not busy
friendly: kind and easy to talk to
general: about the whole, not the parts
glass: you see through it, or a cup
government: the people who run a country
grey: the colour between black and white
hat: you wear it on your head
head: бас
the top part of your body
noun
hello: you say it when you meet
help: to make it easier for someone
imagine: to see it in your mind
index: a list at the end of a book
interesting: it makes you want to know more
introduce: to tell people who someone is
job: the work you are paid for
keep: to hold and not give back
key: it opens a lock, or the answers
kind: a type, or nice to people
know: to have something in your head
left: the side, or past of leave
leg: you walk on two of them
lend: to give for a short time
letter: a written message, or a, b, c
lift: a box that carries you up, or to raise
lose: to not find, or not win
luggage: bags you take on a trip
machine: a thing with parts that works
management: the people who run the company
manager: the person who runs the work
mark: to write a sign, or a grade
match: to pair up, or a game
material: what a thing is made of
matter: a topic, or to be important
miss: to feel sad without, or to not catch
morning: the early part of the day
movement: moving, or a group with a goal
name: аты
what you call someone
noun
neighbour: the person living next to you
neither: not one and not the other
notice: to see it, or a sign
object: a thing, or to say no
offer: to say you will give it
oh: a sound you make when surprised
oil: a thick liquid for cooking or fuel
order: to ask for goods, or the sequence
page: one side of paper in a book
park: green land, or to leave a car
pass: to go by, or to hand over
passport: a book that lets you travel
patient: a sick person, or calm waiting
phone: you talk to people on it
phrasal: made of two or more words
piano: a big music instrument with keys
pick: to choose, or to take up
planet: a big body going round a star
pocket: a small bag in your clothes
point: a fact, or to show with a finger
position: where it is, or a job
preposition: a word like in, on, at
pretty: nice to look at, or fairly
promise: to say you will surely do it
pronoun: a word like I, you, it
put: to move a thing to a place
question: сұрақ
you ask it to learn
noun
read: to look at words and understand
record: facts kept, or to save sound
remember: to keep it in your mind
rent: to pay to use a home
replace: to put a new one in its place
resign: to leave your job by choice
rest: a break, or what is left
return: to come back, or give back
revise: to study again before a test
ring: to phone, or jewellery for a finger
run: to move fast on your feet
sack: to make someone leave a job
save: to keep, or to not waste
science: the study of how things work
second: after the first, or a tiny time
send: to make it go to someone
sentence: words that make a full idea
set: a group, or to put in place
shoes: you wear them on your feet
sign: a mark, or to write your name
snow: soft white ice from the sky
someone: a person you do not name
something: a thing you do not name
sorry: a word for saying you feel bad
sort: a kind, or to put in order
state: a country, or how it is
statement: a sentence that says a fact
steal: to take what is not yours
stress: the strong part of a word, or worry
subject: пән; тақырып
what you study or talk about
noun
suggest: to say an idea for someone
suit: matching clothes, or to look right
sun: the star that gives us light
sunday: the last day of the week
suppose: to think it is probably so
taste: what food is like in your mouth
taxi: a car you pay to ride in
tell: to give news to a person
temperature: how hot or cold it is
term: part of a school year, or a word
test: questions to check what you know
thanks: you say it for a favour
the: points to a thing you already know
through: in one side and out the other
throw: to send it through the air
to: shows where you go, or starts a verb
tooth: white and hard, in your mouth
travel: to go from place to place
travelling: going from place to place now
tree: a tall plant with a trunk
turn: to change direction, or your go
type: a kind, or to write on a keyboard
underline: to draw a line under a word
use: to do a job with a thing
using: doing a job with a thing
very: a lot, to a high degree
view: what you see, or an opinion
warning: words that tell you of danger
watch: to look at, or a clock
whether: if, one way or the other
work: a job, or to do a job
write: to make words with a pen
york: a city in England; also New York
you: the person I am talking to
